name: Some Natural Phenomena
subject: physics
summary: Lightning, earthquakes, and thunderstorms — the science behind nature's most dramatic events and how to stay safe.

crushed.title: Lightning = massive spark
crushed.text: Charge builds in clouds → jumps to ground or another cloud. Heats air to ~30,000°C — thunder is the shockwave.

crushed.title: Earthquake = plate movement
crushed.text: Tectonic plates shift suddenly. Seismic waves shake ground. Richter scale measures magnitude.

crushed.title: Lightning safety
crushed.text: Stay indoors, avoid trees/water/metal. Car is safe (Faraday cage). Squat low in open field.

crushed.title: Earthquake safety
crushed.text: Under sturdy table, away from windows. Open field if outside. Don't use lifts.

crushed.title: Thunderstorm
crushed.text: Rising warm air → cumulonimbus cloud → rain + lightning + thunder + sometimes hail.

items.label: Charge separation
items.detail: Ice particles in cloud collide — positive top, negative bottom

items.label: Induction
items.detail: Negative cloud base repels electrons in ground — positive charge on surface

items.label: Discharge
items.detail: When potential difference is huge, charge jumps — lightning bolt

items.label: Thunder
items.detail: Air heated instantly expands explosively — sonic boom we hear as thunder

items.label: Epicentre
items.detail: Point on surface directly above focus — most damage here

items.label: Richter scale
items.detail: Logarithmic scale — each step = 10× more amplitude

flows.title: How Lightning Strikes
flows.description: From charge buildup in a cloud to the bolt hitting the ground.

steps.label: Cloud
steps.description: Positive charges top, negative charges bottom of thundercloud.

steps.label: Ground
steps.description: Negative cloud base pushes electrons away — ground becomes positively charged.

steps.label: Leader
steps.description: Negative charge creeps downward in jagged steps toward ground.

steps.label: Return stroke
steps.description: Positive charge rushes up to meet it — the bright flash we see.

steps.label: Thunder
steps.description: Channel heated to 30,000°C — air explodes outward as thunder.

flows.title: How Seismic Waves Travel
flows.description: Earthquake at focus sends waves through Earth — seismograph detects them.

steps.label: Plate slip
steps.description: Tectonic plates suddenly slip at a fault line.

steps.label: P-waves
steps.description: Primary waves travel fastest — arrive first at seismograph.

steps.label: S-waves
steps.description: Secondary waves arrive second — cause more shaking damage.

steps.label: Surface
steps.description: Waves reach surface — buildings shake most at epicentre.

steps.label: Seismograph
steps.description: Instrument records wave amplitude — Richter magnitude calculated.

keyTerms.definition: Metal rod on buildings that safely directs lightning to ground
keyTerms.analogy: A lightning escape route for buildings

keyTerms.definition: Instrument that detects and records seismic waves
Giant ocean wave caused by undersea earthquake

demos.title: Lightning & Thunder
demos.description: Charge builds in clouds → lightning discharges → thunder follows.

steps.label: Charge separation
steps.description: Ice particles in clouds separate positive and negative charges.

steps.label: Lightning strike
steps.description: Huge voltage jumps — bright flash heats air to ~30,000°C.

steps.label: Thunder
steps.description: Heated air expands explosively — sound arrives seconds later.

chapterSummary.overview: This chapter explains natural events involving static electricity (charging by rubbing, types of charges, lightning) and earthquakes (causes, measurement on Richter scale, safety measures). It connects physics concepts to real-world phenomena we observe in nature.
keyPoints: Like charges repel; unlike charges attract; charge transfers by rubbing (e.g. comb and hair).
Lightning: charge separation in clouds → discharge → thunder from heated air expanding.
Lightning conductor protects buildings by providing a safe path to ground.
Earthquakes caused by movement of tectonic plates; measured on Richter scale.
Seismic zones in India; safety: take cover under sturdy furniture, stay away from windows.
chapterSummary.examFocus: Explain lightning formation. Draw a lightning conductor. List earthquake safety rules.
